reasoning: The informal statement defines "isomorphism" parenthetically as "there exists a two-sided inverse map φ⁻¹: G' → G". The Lean encoding uses `∃ φ₁ : G' → G, Function.LeftInverse φ₁ φ ∧ Function.RightInverse φ₁ φ`, which precisely captures a two-sided inverse function (φ₁ ∘ φ = id and φ ∘ φ₁ = id). The word "map" in the informal text most naturally reads as "function", matching the Lean choice of `G' → G` rather than `G' →* G`. Importantly, for group homomorphisms, having a two-sided inverse function is equivalent to having a two-sided inverse homomorphism (the inverse is automatically a homomorphism in the group setting), so both readings are logically equivalent. The right side of the iff (`Injective φ ∧ Surjective φ`) directly matches "injective and surjective". The iff structure and group-homomorphism setup are preserved correctly.
